openFindings.title: Folder boost demotes scoped hits by capping 0-100 similarity at 1.0
openFindings.scopeProof: The issue is in memory_search folder ranking, a search/query handler under the declared search/retrieval MCP server scope.
affectedSurfaceHints: memory_search calls with folderBoost from memory_context folder discovery
ranking order and formatted similarity for returned search results

claimAdjudication.claim: Folder boost can invert intended ranking because it multiplies raw 0-100 similarity and caps boosted in-folder rows at 1.0 before sorting by similarity descending.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Checked the test exercising applyFolderBoostRanking and found it uses 0.7/0.6 similarity inputs, which do not match the documented and produced 0-100 production contract.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: If all callers supplied already-normalized 0-1 similarity, the cap would be valid; formatter and pipeline contracts show that is not the handler's production contract.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the production rows reaching applyFolderBoostRanking are proven normalized to 0-1 on every path before this function runs.
